echo (Energy and Commodity Holistic Optimiser)
  • »
  • echo API »
  • echo.echo_models module
  • View page source

echo.echo_models module

Models for Echo components: Port, Node, Edge, Transform, Graph and Path.

This module contains Base models definition and a library of predefined commodity specific models (Electrical, Thermal, Gas, Carbon)

class echo.echo_models.BoundedElectricalLoad(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 3, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, upper_bound: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, lower_bound: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType)

Bases: echo.echo_models.ElectricalPort

A port where the load has to be within a max and min value which is specified at each timestep.

initialise_port(model)

Creates pyomo vars, params, and constraints for the port

Parameters

model – pyomo concrete model

class echo.echo_models.CarbonAggregation(*, node_name: str = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 0, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, sum: echo.echo_models.BaseModel = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Node

class echo.echo_models.CarbonSink(*, units: int = 2, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 1, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Port

For sinking carbon emissions into an aggregation node

class echo.echo_models.CarbonSinkNode(*, node_name: str = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 2, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Node

Bases: echo.echo_models.Port

For doing carbon emissions from an asset (node)

class echo.echo_models.Chiller(*, node_name: str = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 2, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, max_input: echo.echo_models.ConstrainedFloatValue, max_output: echo.echo_models.ConstrainedFloatValue, input_pts: dict = None, output_pts: dict = None, temp_coef: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType = None, input_coef: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType = None, n_pts: int = 5, temperature_array: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.InputOutputPiecewiseNode

A chiller converts an electrical input to a thermal cooling output.

exception echo.echo_models.ConfigurationError

Bases: Exception

class echo.echo_models.ControlledGen(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 2, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, min_utilisation: float = None, max_utilisation: float = None, max_power: echo.echo_models.ConstrainedFloatValue, min_power: echo.echo_models.ConstrainedFloatValue)

Bases: echo.echo_models.ControlledLoadOrGen

class echo.echo_models.ControlledLoad(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 1, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, min_utilisation: float = None, max_utilisation: float = None, max_power: echo.echo_models.ConstrainedFloatValue, min_power: echo.echo_models.ConstrainedFloatValue)

Bases: echo.echo_models.ControlledLoadOrGen

class echo.echo_models.ControlledLoadOrGen(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 3, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, min_utilisation: float = None, max_utilisation: float = None, max_power: float = None, min_power: float = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.FlexPort

A controlled load or generation has a max/min power, as well as a max/min utilisation. The load/generation must be operated within the min and max utilisation (per time unit).

initialise_port(model)

Creates pyomo vars, params, and constraints for the port

Parameters

model – pyomo concrete model

Base models

alias of TypeVar(‘pandas.core.frame.DataFrame’)

class echo.echo_models.Demand(*, units: int = 0, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 1, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 1, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Sink

class echo.echo_models.EV(*, node_name: str = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 0, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, units: int = 1, charge_mode: str = None, available: Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, list], usage: Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, list], cp_name: str = 'cp', tod_charging: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, list]] = None, interval_duration: int, max_capacity: float, depth_of_discharge_limit: float = 0, charging_power_limit: float, discharging_power_limit: float, charging_efficiency: float = 1, discharging_efficiency: float = 1, initial_state_of_charge: float, trip_slack: bool = False, soc_conserv: float = None, soc_conserv_cost: float = None, V0G_delta: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, list]] = None, V0G_SOC: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, list]] = None, V0G_trip_infeasibility: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, list]] = None, charge_status: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.ElectricalNode

V0G_charging(interval_duration, force_conv=False)

Convert V0G vehicle (convenience charging) to a soc profile and a power profile if possible.

class echo.echo_models.Edge(*, uid: uuid.UUID = None, edge_name: str = None, opt_type: int = 0, vertices: tuple, tariff: list = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.BaseModel

Edges are used to connect nodes. For an edge (x, y) where x and y are nodes, the edge value is equal to the flow from x->y plus the flow from y->x.

add_vertices(obj1, obj2)

Adds edge vertices (which are ports) :param obj1: port object :param obj2: port object

initialise_edge(model)

Applies edge constraint: port1 = -1 *port2 :param model: pyomo concrete model

class echo.echo_models.ElectricalDemand(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 1, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 1, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, can_be_shed: bool = False, shed_cost: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType = None, is_off: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Sink

Fixed electrical demand

add_objective(model)

Adds port-specific objective terms to pyomo model :param model: pyomo concrete model

initialise_port(model)

Creates pyomo vars, params, and constraints for the port

model – pyomo concrete model

class echo.echo_models.ElectricalGeneration(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 1, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 2, import_constraint: int = 0, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, curtailable: bool = False, port_name_max: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Source

Electrical generation which can be fixed (non-curtailable) or variable (curtailable)

class echo.echo_models.ElectricalNode(*, node_name: str = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 0, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, units: int = 1)

class echo.echo_models.ElectricalPort(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 3, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.FlexPort

Flexible electrical port

class echo.echo_models.ElectricalStorage(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 3, import_constraint: int = 2, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 2, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, max_capacity: float, depth_of_discharge_limit: float = 0, min_soc: float = 0, charging_power_limit: float, discharging_power_limit: float, charging_efficiency: float = 1, discharging_efficiency: float = 1, initial_state_of_charge: float, fixed_storage_capacity: bool = True, storage_capacity_cost: pydantic.types.PositiveFloat = None, var_opex: float = 0.0, regularise: bool = False, enable_trip_slack: bool = False, soc_conserv: float = None, soc_conserv_cost: float = None, available: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, list]] = None, soc_value: str = None, optimised_capacity: str = None, trip_slack: str = None, cons_slack: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Storage

class echo.echo_models.FixedElectricalPort(*, units: int = 1, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 1, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 3, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.ElectricalPort

An electrical port with fixed values (parameters).

class echo.echo_models.FlexPort(*, units: int = 0, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 3, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 1, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Port

Flexible port

class echo.echo_models.GasBoiler(*, node_name: str = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 0, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, gas_to_heat_efficiency: float)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Node

Gas boiler converts gas to heat at a fixed COP.

class echo.echo_models.GasDemand

Bases: echo.echo_models.Sink

class echo.echo_models.HCLoad

Bases: echo.echo_models.Sink

Heating or cooling load

class echo.echo_models.HeatPump(*, node_name: str = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 2, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, max_input: echo.echo_models.ConstrainedFloatValue, max_output: echo.echo_models.ConstrainedFloatValue, input_pts: dict = None, output_pts: dict = None, temp_coef: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType = None, input_coef: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType = None, n_pts: int = 5, temperature_array: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.InputOutputPiecewiseNode

A heat pump converts an electrical input to hot water which can be used for heating, and chilled water which can be used for cooling.

class echo.echo_models.InputOutputPiecewiseNode(*, node_name: str = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 2, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, max_input: echo.echo_models.ConstrainedFloatValue, max_output: echo.echo_models.ConstrainedFloatValue, input_pts: dict = None, output_pts: dict = None, temp_coef: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType = None, input_coef: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType = None, n_pts: int = 5, temperature_array: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Node

A node with one input and one output. The transformation between input and output is defined by an array of input and output pts, which are used to construct a linear piecewise function. Alternatively, the transformation can be defined by an array of coefficients of a polynomial function.

get_cop(optimiser)

Returns the coefficient of performance (output/input)

initialise_node(model)

Creating and modifying variables

class echo.echo_models.Inverter(*, node_name: str = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 2, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, units: int = 1, max_import: float = None, max_export: float = None, dc_ac_efficiency: echo.echo_models.ConstrainedFloatValue, ac_dc_efficiency: echo.echo_models.ConstrainedFloatValue, dc_ports: dict = {}, ac_port_name: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.ElectricalNode

An inverter is a node with one AC port and at least one DC port. Flows from AC to DC, and DC to AC, are subject to conversion efficiencies.

class echo.echo_models.Node(*, node_name: str = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 0, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.BaseModel

Nodes are collections of one or more ports that can include non-trivial relationships between the ports, this allows transformations to be implemented.

add_emission_transformation(emitting_port, carbon_port, emission_factor)

Creates an emission transformation and adds to the node. :param emitting_port: port object that generates emissions when exporting (when negative) :param carbon_port: port object that represents carbon flows :param emission_factor: a ratio = emissions generated/emitting unit generated (float)

add_flex_port(name, unit=0)

Adds named port of specified type to node. :param name: port name as string :param unit: Unit

add_transformation(transformation_obj)

Adds a transformation object to a node. :param transformation_obj: Transform

class echo.echo_models.OptimisationGraph

Bases: networkx.classes.graph.Graph

create_path_objects(sources, sinks, regularise=False)

Creates path objects according to source/sink lists provided.

draw(with_labels=False, labels=None)

Draws the network with or without node labels

get_ports_on_edge_from_nodes(node1, node2)

Gets the ports that are on the edge from node1 to node2.

get_sources_and_sinks()

Returns a set that contains all source and sink nodes.

lookup_node_from_port(port)

Returns node that a specified port belongs to, if the port belongs to a node.

print_network_hierarchy()

Prints the model hierarchy as node names –> port names

verify_paths()

Verifies that our paths meet the assumptions required to correctly do flow tracing.

class echo.echo_models.Path(*, edge_ports: List[tuple] = [], vertices: list, uid: uuid.UUID = None, path_name: str = None, regularise: bool = False, flow_value: str = None, contingency_neg: str = None, contingency_pos: str = None, path_tariff: str = None, slack: str = None, units: int = 1)

Bases: echo.echo_models.BaseModel

A path is a sequence of distinct vertices (nodes).

class echo.echo_models.Sink(*, units: int = 0, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 1, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 1, import_constraint: int = 1, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Port

The sink for a commodity.

class echo.echo_models.Source(*, units: int = 0, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 1, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 2, import_constraint: int = 0, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 0, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Port

A source of a commodity.

class echo.echo_models.Storage(*, units: int = 0, initial_value: dict = 0.0, opt_type: int = 2, uid: uuid.UUID = None, port_name: str = None, flows: int = 3, import_constraint: int = 2, import_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, export_constraint: int = 2, export_constraint_value: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, float]] = None, active_periods: dict = None, slack: bool = False, optional: bool = False, import_con_val: str = None, export_con_val: str = None, import_slack: str = None, import_slack_max: str = None, export_slack: str = None, export_slack_max: str = None, pos: str = None, is_pos: str = None, neg: str = None, active: str = None, max_capacity: float, depth_of_discharge_limit: float = 0, min_soc: float = 0, charging_power_limit: float, discharging_power_limit: float, charging_efficiency: float = 1, discharging_efficiency: float = 1, initial_state_of_charge: float, fixed_storage_capacity: bool = True, storage_capacity_cost: pydantic.types.PositiveFloat = None, var_opex: float = 0.0, regularise: bool = False, enable_trip_slack: bool = False, soc_conserv: float = None, soc_conserv_cost: float = None, available: Optional[Union[echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, list]] = None, soc_value: str = None, optimised_capacity: str = None, trip_slack: str = None, cons_slack: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Port

Storage for a commodity.

add_objective(model)

Adds port-specific objective terms to pyomo model :param model: pyomo concrete model

classmethod dod_check(values)

Validator for depth of discharge.

initialise_port(model)

Creates pyomo vars, params, and constraints for the port

Parameters

model – pyomo concrete model

class echo.echo_models.TellegenNode(*, node_name: str = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 1, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Node

A node that implements a Tellegen constraint requiring that port values sum to zero.

class echo.echo_models.TimeDelayNode(*, node_name: str = None, uid: uuid.UUID = None, ports: dict = {}, node_rule: int = 2, transformations: dict = {}, named_ports: list = [], inflow: str = None, time_delay: float)

Bases: echo.echo_models.Node

An input output node that implements a fixed delay between input and output.

class echo.echo_models.Transform(*, uid: uuid.UUID = None, transform_name: str = None, rhs: list = [], lhs: list = [])

Bases: echo.echo_models.BaseModel

An object for carrying a generic linear node transformation.

add_lhs_term(var, rule, weight)

Adds a left hand side (RHS) term to the transform :param var: pyomo var name :param rule: TransformationRule object :param weight: linear factor (float)

add_rhs_term(var, rule, weight)

Adds a right hand side (RHS) term to the transform :param var: pyomo var name :param rule: TransformationRule object :param weight: linear factor (float)
